title: the night i prayed for geese
subtitle: at a sunday easter service

blocks.content: With finals season looming, I did what any rational person would do: I went on sidequests. Here were my top 3 for the week:
1. I played Tetris Jenga at the club.

Well, it wasn’t really a club. It was more of an obscure restaurant tucked in the basement of this incredibly old clock tower building. It’s called the Grad Club, and what I love most about it is how perfectly ironic a place like it could exist in a building like that. I think of it as a physical manifestation of what it would feel like to know your grandpa used to party a lot.

I probably would never have discovered this place had it not been for our debate club. They made it a tradition to go after Thursday practices each week. As people were waiting for their food, I went to the shelf of board games at the back. It was filled to the brim with every board game you could think of. In fact, it had all the board games I usually play: Exploding Kittens, Monopoly, Coup, and all the Catan expansions.

As I was browsing through the options, a box caught my eye. For those who haven’t played Tetris Jenga, it’s basically in the name. Jenga with Tetris pieces. I brought it over to the table where everyone was sitting. I opened the box. And for the next 2 hours, I was lost in the world of Tetris shapes and increasingly precarious architecture.

My ego still runs high from that night, when by the end of one game, with no more options left, I did the impossible: I took out two supporting beams near the bottom without the entire thing collapsing.
2. I took pictures with my camera instead of eating dinner.

I don’t condone this, but after getting (and finishing) a $50 Uber Eats order for lunch late into the afternoon, I didn’t feel so hungry. I promise I don’t do this on a daily basis. It was my second time ever ordering, and I’m probably never ordering again. Also, I was actually going to eat dinner, but I didn’t know the caf had already closed at 5:30 that day because of Easter Weekend.

As I was contemplating whether I should open my stash of instant noodles, I looked out the window. The night sky was pitch black, with small lights dotting the darkness in the distance. Looking down, I saw the lamps that revealed bits and pieces of the road circling my residence.

Tonight’s view was perfect. So perfect that I decided right now was the best time to start my hobby of photography and take pictures. Snapping the first photo, I realized something I’m sure everyone realizes after taking a photo through a window at night: I saw my reflection in the picture. But I decided to just go with it and started finding other things and places I could get a reflection from. I definitely got a weird look from a girl, though, who was filling her water bottle when I was taking pictures of my reflection in the microwave. I think the ego from Tetris Jenga got to me.
3. I went to a Sunday Easter Service even though I’m not religious.

I gotta say, it was nice talking and seeing genuine people after being alone in my room for the past three days. Everyone had decided to leave and spend Easter with their families. That might have been another reason I decided to take pictures now that I think about it.

I met some really cool people. I sang a few worship songs. I participated in the communion (they even had gluten-free bread for it). And I learned that Shona, the most spoken language in Zimbabwe, had many similarities to Japanese.
We finished with a prayer circle, where I prayed for the geese that chased me when I was walking to the service.

It reminded me a lot of my childhood. Not the geese part, but the service as a whole. Despite not being religious, I went to a Christian elementary school. I had chapel on Wednesdays, where I sang many of the same worship songs. We always prayed at the end of class each day. And of course, I always admired the incredible architecture of those churches (I wonder if they have a club in the basement).
So, should you go sidequesting during finals?
To be honest, I don’t know.
But what I can tell you is this:
You won’t forget the second you successfully pull out those two Tetris Jenga blocks.
You won’t regret the instant before you press the shutter of the camera, knowing you captured the perfect shot.
And, you’ll definitely love the moment you hear your prayer circle chuckle at your prayers for those geese.
